CREATE TABLE registry_settings (setting_name VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY, setting_value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT * FROM (SELECT setting_name, setting_value FROM registry_settings) AS temp_table;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT setting_name, setting_value FROM registry_settings) AS distinct_settings;
SELECT setting_name, setting_value FROM registry_settings WHERE setting_name LIKE 'setting%';
SELECT * FROM registry_settings ORDER BY setting_name DESC;
UPDATE registry_settings SET setting_value = 'new_value1' WHERE setting_name = 'setting1';
SELECT setting_name, setting_value FROM registry_settings WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM registry_settings WHERE setting_value = 'value2');
SELECT setting_name, setting_value FROM registry_settings UNION SELECT 'fallback_setting', 'fallback_value';
SELECT setting_name, setting_value FROM (SELECT * FROM registry_settings WHERE setting_value IS NOT NULL) AS settings_with_value;

SELECT setting_name FROM registry_settings WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM registry_settings AS b WHERE b.setting_name = registry_settings.setting_name AND b.setting_value = registry_settings.setting_value);
WITH setting_counts AS (SELECT setting_name, COUNT(*) AS count FROM registry_settings GROUP BY setting_name) SELECT setting_name FROM setting_counts WHERE count > 1;
